title: Solving the energy conundrum is key to unlocking the UK’s AI economy
excerpt: Flexible energy contracts could power the UK’s AI growth
content_html:
Recent evidence shows that energy cost pressures and a power grid capacity crunch risk becoming a bottleneck on the growth of the UK’s digital economy.

A report by Oxford Economics for the Nuclear Industry Association warns that growing grid capacity constraints and uncompetitive industrial electricity prices could drive data center developers overseas.

This comes as data center energy demands could increase fivefold by 2035 and data center operators face growing pressure for more sustainable energy in line with climate targets.

Head of UK Corporate Account Management at SEFE Energy UK.

This is driving many data center developers to explore alternative supply sources such as flexible contracts and Corporate Power Purchase Agreements (CPPAs) which offer affordable, secure long-term power.

Yet many data center developers lack the resources to navigate these complex contracts or meet the high credit requirements.

There is an urgent need for creative new solutions to provide affordable sustainable power for our digital economy.

The energy chokepoint for the AI economy

The Government aims to make Britain the fastest AI-adopting country in the G7 and this will require accelerated data center expansion with AI data centers being prioritized for new demand connections to the grid. While this is welcome, demand-side connections will not alleviate the supply-side challenges from rising electricity costs to network capacity constraints.

Sign up to the TechRadar Pro newsletter to get all the top news, opinion, features and guidance your business needs to succeed!

Data centers have build times of 12-14 months yet large new renewable energy projects can face waits of 12-14 years to come online at a time when Britain faces rising non-commodity electricity costs such as Transmission Network Use of System (TNUoS) and Nuclear Regulated Asset Base (RAB) charges to fund new electric grid and nuclear energy infrastructure.

Ofgem has warned that data center power consumption could significantly exceed Britain’s current peak electricity consumption, risking rising energy costs.

The move towards flexible contracts

Flexible electricity contracts that allow companies to buy energy in chunks offer a potential solution to this by allowing data centers to tailor energy costs to their consumption.

Crucially, flexible contracts can be adjusted to hedge against fluctuating energy consumption for facilities such as AI data centers which have more variable patterns of energy use. This would also help avoid penalties for ramping up energy use to take on major new customers.

While fixed-price contracts can lock in long-term energy costs to offer certainty, flexible contracts enable data centers to take advantage of price fluctuations to secure cheaper power.

For example, we have a dedicated pricing team monitoring market movements round the clock. This could help facilities partially hedge against the risk of rising prices, setting a price for a portion of their consumption, while buying the rest when required to take advantage of favorable prices on the spot market.

For example, we implemented a flexible contract for an energy-intensive industrial chemicals company which included a cash-out arrangement, enabling them to lock in prices when costs are low and buy power in batches days or even months ahead as needed.

There are various kinds of flexible contract options based on the degree to which companies can forecast their energy consumption. For example, ‘tolerance banding’ which guarantees a set price within a certain range or ‘band’ of electricity consumption, can provide certainty amidst unpredictable, fluctuating costs.

As data center operators become more confident in forecasting energy consumption, this can create even cheaper options such as contracts that enable them to buy every kilowatt-hour above or below expected demand.

The CPPA model

Other contracts such as CPPAs could offer data centers a secure, sustainable, affordable power supply directly from suppliers at stable cost. Private-wire PPAs involving onsite generation could enable data centers to sell surplus power back to the grid, transforming energy from a cost into a revenue stream. On site generation could also help avoid the non-commodity costs for grid electricity such as TNUoS charges that comprise 60% of electricity bills and are set to increase to fund new infrastructure.

With targets to reduce operational emissions from buildings by 76%, CPPAs also help facilities meet climate targets by providing traceable green power. As large consumers with a relatively stable long-term demand, data centers are perfectly placed to tap into this market. Amidst growing energy price volatility and security risks, fixed-price CPPAs offer the chance to lock in power prices and supplies, providing certainty and security.

The barriers to alternative contracts

Yet there are many barriers to flexible contracts and CPPAs market for smaller entities such as data centers. These contracts can be highly complex, contain stringent credit requirements and often require 10-15 year agreements. The Contracts for Difference (CfD) scheme, which gives renewable developers a government-backed route to market through fixed long-term price support, can also act as a competing route to market for generators.

In some cases, this can make long-term corporate offtake agreements less attractive, particularly where developers can secure greater certainty through the CfD mechanism. For smaller customers seeking greener electricity, however, this route can offer an alternative when a direct CPPA may be harder to access.

Many data center projects are run by startups or smaller entities that lack the resources for such long-term commitments or credit requirements. Data centers also present a slightly higher credit risk than other facilities because their revenue streams are not based on a few large, long-term customers but split among many customers across the digital economy.

Opening the market to data centers

There is an urgent need for creative solutions to lower barriers to entry to the flexible contract market for smaller entities such as data centers. Security deposits or bank guarantees can help some firms meet the stringent credit requirements.

Other potential solutions include parent-company guarantees where a parent company makes a commitment to cover the cost in the event of a default or intercompany guarantees where large data center customers such as Amazon offer guarantees.

There are also solutions to simplify adoption and help CPPAs slot into existing energy use. For example, PPA import sleeving contracts, where energy is pre-purchased from a generator or utility and supplied directly to a facility through the grid, can help alleviate energy costs and security risks.

Tailoring contracts to specific energy needs

Independent partners can also help data centers optimize contracts for their specific energy needs and financial situation. Independent brokers can consolidate the process of negotiating with generators, suppliers and investors, speeding up and de-risking adoption.

Brokers can also help negotiate and monitor contracts suited to the precise profile of each data center. For example, cloud computing data centers with relatively steady, predictable demand may prefer fixed contracts whereas AI data centers processing huge amounts of data for many clients have a more variable, ‘peaky’ pattern of consumption and require flexible contracts.

Data centers can also work with partners to get live market intelligence on changing regulations or market movements. For example, we offered one client an early warning service so that they were able to minimize costs during the winter triads, half-hour periods of peak demand during the winter season.

Towards a new model of data center energy

Britain’s ability to compete in the accelerating AI race increasingly hinges on the ability to mitigate the risk of rising energy costs.

Energy security will also be critical to building truly sovereign AI capabilities for the UK. Lowering the barriers to the flexible contract market could create new opportunities at both ends, providing secure, sustainable and affordable power to unlock data center growth while unlocking vital investment in new renewable energy capacity.

Yet this will require tailored, adaptable contractual models from bespoke flexible contracts to PPAs and new ways of lowering barriers to entry including reducing complexity and stringent credit requirements. This could help provide secure, sustainable and affordable long-term power to fuel our digital economy.

We list the best web hosting services: 60 sites tested to find the 10 fastest, most reliable platforms.

This article was produced as part of TechRadar Pro Perspectives, our channel to feature the best and brightest minds in the technology industry today.

The views expressed here are those of the author and are not necessarily those of TechRadarPro or Future plc. If you are interested in contributing find out more here: https://www.techradar.com/pro/perspectives-how-to-submit

blocks.html_en: Recent evidence shows that energy cost pressures and a power grid capacity crunch risk becoming a bottleneck on the growth of the UK’s digital economy.

sentences.en: Recent evidence shows that energy cost pressures and a power grid capacity crunch risk becoming a bottleneck on the growth of the UK’s digital economy.
sentences.zh: 最近的证据表明，能源成本压力和电网容量紧缩有可能成为英国数字经济增长的瓶颈。

blocks.html_en: A report by Oxford Economics for the Nuclear Industry Association warns that growing grid capacity constraints and uncompetitive industrial electricity prices could drive data center developers overseas.

sentences.en: A report by Oxford Economics for the Nuclear Industry Association warns that growing grid capacity constraints and uncompetitive industrial electricity prices could drive data center developers overseas.
sentences.zh: 牛津经济研究院为核工业协会撰写的一份报告警告称，日益严重的电网容量限制和缺乏竞争力的工业电价可能会促使数据中心开发商转向海外。

blocks.html_en: This comes as data center energy demands could increase fivefold by 2035 and data center operators face growing pressure for more sustainable energy in line with climate targets.

sentences.en: This comes as data center energy demands could increase fivefold by 2035 and data center operators face growing pressure for more sustainable energy in line with climate targets.
sentences.zh: 到 2035 年，数据中心的能源需求可能会增加五倍，而数据中心运营商面临着越来越大的压力，要求提供符合气候目标的可持续能源。

blocks.html_en: Head of UK Corporate Account Management at SEFE Energy UK.

sentences.en: Head of UK Corporate Account Management at SEFE Energy UK.
sentences.zh: SEFE Energy UK 的英国企业客户管理主管。

blocks.html_en: This is driving many data center developers to explore alternative supply sources such as flexible contracts and Corporate Power Purchase Agreements (CPPAs) which offer affordable, secure long-term power.

sentences.en: This is driving many data center developers to explore alternative supply sources such as flexible contracts and Corporate Power Purchase Agreements (CPPAs) which offer affordable, secure long-term power.
sentences.zh: 这促使许多数据中心开发商探索替代供应来源，例如灵活的合同和企业购电协议（CPPA），以提供负担得起的、安全的长期电力。

blocks.html_en: Yet many data center developers lack the resources to navigate these complex contracts or meet the high credit requirements.

sentences.en: Yet many data center developers lack the resources to navigate these complex contracts or meet the high credit requirements.
sentences.zh: 然而，许多数据中心开发商缺乏资源来处理这些复杂的合同或满足高信用要求。

blocks.html_en: There is an urgent need for creative new solutions to provide affordable sustainable power for our digital economy.

sentences.en: There is an urgent need for creative new solutions to provide affordable sustainable power for our digital economy.
sentences.zh: 迫切需要创造性的新解决方案，为我们的数字经济提供负担得起的可持续电力。

blocks.html_en: The energy chokepoint for the AI economy

sentences.en: The energy chokepoint for the AI economy
sentences.zh: 人工智能经济的能源瓶颈

blocks.html_en: The Government aims to make Britain the fastest AI-adopting country in the G7 and this will require accelerated data center expansion with AI data centers being prioritized for new demand connections to the grid. While this is welcome, demand-side connections will not alleviate the supply-side challenges from rising electricity costs to network capacity constraints.

sentences.en: The Government aims to make Britain the fastest AI-adopting country in the G7 and this will require accelerated data center expansion with AI data centers being prioritized for new demand connections to the grid.
sentences.zh: 政府的目标是使英国成为七国集团中采用人工智能最快的国家，这将需要加速数据中心的扩张，并优先考虑人工智能数据中心与电网的新需求连接。

sentences.en: While this is welcome, demand-side connections will not alleviate the supply-side challenges from rising electricity costs to network capacity constraints.
sentences.zh: 虽然这值得欢迎，但需求方的连接并不能缓解供应方面临的挑战，如电力成本上升和网络容量限制等。

blocks.html_en: Data centers have build times of 12-14 months yet large new renewable energy projects can face waits of 12-14 years to come online at a time when Britain faces rising non-commodity electricity costs such as Transmission Network Use of System (TNUoS) and Nuclear Regulated Asset Base (RAB) charges to fund new electric grid and nuclear energy infrastructure.

sentences.en: Data centers have build times of 12-14 months yet large new renewable energy projects can face waits of 12-14 years to come online at a time when Britain faces rising non-commodity electricity costs such as Transmission Network Use of System (TNUoS) and Nuclear Regulated Asset Base (RAB) charges to fund new electric grid and nuclear energy infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 数据中心的建设时间为 12 至 14 个月，但大型新可再生能源项目可能需要等待 12 至 14 年才能上线，而此时英国面临非商品电力成本不断上涨，例如传输网络使用系统 (TNUoS) 和核监管资产基础 (RAB) 费用，以资助新的电网和核能基础设施。

blocks.html_en: Ofgem has warned that data center power consumption could significantly exceed Britain’s current peak electricity consumption, risking rising energy costs.

sentences.en: Ofgem has warned that data center power consumption could significantly exceed Britain’s current peak electricity consumption, risking rising energy costs.
sentences.zh: Ofgem警告说，数据中心的电力消耗可能会大大超过英国目前的电力消耗峰值，从而带来能源成本上升的风险。

blocks.html_en: The move towards flexible contracts

sentences.en: The move towards flexible contracts
sentences.zh: 转向灵活合同

blocks.html_en: Flexible electricity contracts that allow companies to buy energy in chunks offer a potential solution to this by allowing data centers to tailor energy costs to their consumption.

sentences.en: Flexible electricity contracts that allow companies to buy energy in chunks offer a potential solution to this by allowing data centers to tailor energy costs to their consumption.
sentences.zh: 灵活的电力合同允许公司批量购买能源，这为数据中心根据其消耗量调整能源成本提供了一个潜在的解决方案。

blocks.html_en: Crucially, flexible contracts can be adjusted to hedge against fluctuating energy consumption for facilities such as AI data centers which have more variable patterns of energy use. This would also help avoid penalties for ramping up energy use to take on major new customers.

sentences.en: Crucially, flexible contracts can be adjusted to hedge against fluctuating energy consumption for facilities such as AI data centers which have more variable patterns of energy use.
sentences.zh: 至关重要的是，可以调整灵活的合同，以对冲人工智能数据中心等能源使用模式更加多样化的设施的能源消耗波动。

sentences.en: This would also help avoid penalties for ramping up energy use to take on major new customers.
sentences.zh: 这也将有助于避免因增加能源使用量来吸引主要新客户而受到处罚。

blocks.html_en: While fixed-price contracts can lock in long-term energy costs to offer certainty, flexible contracts enable data centers to take advantage of price fluctuations to secure cheaper power.

sentences.en: While fixed-price contracts can lock in long-term energy costs to offer certainty, flexible contracts enable data centers to take advantage of price fluctuations to secure cheaper power.
sentences.zh: 虽然固定价格合同可以锁定长期能源成本以提供确定性，但灵活的合同使数据中心能够利用价格波动来获得更便宜的电力。

blocks.html_en: For example, we have a dedicated pricing team monitoring market movements round the clock. This could help facilities partially hedge against the risk of rising prices, setting a price for a portion of their consumption, while buying the rest when required to take advantage of favorable prices on the spot market.

sentences.en: For example, we have a dedicated pricing team monitoring market movements round the clock.
sentences.zh: 例如，我们有一个专门的定价团队全天候监控市场走势。

sentences.en: This could help facilities partially hedge against the risk of rising prices, setting a price for a portion of their consumption, while buying the rest when required to take advantage of favorable prices on the spot market.
sentences.zh: 这可以帮助设施部分对冲价格上涨的风险，为部分消费设定价格，同时在需要时购买其余部分，以利用现货市场上的优惠价格。

blocks.html_en: For example, we implemented a flexible contract for an energy-intensive industrial chemicals company which included a cash-out arrangement, enabling them to lock in prices when costs are low and buy power in batches days or even months ahead as needed.

sentences.en: For example, we implemented a flexible contract for an energy-intensive industrial chemicals company which included a cash-out arrangement, enabling them to lock in prices when costs are low and buy power in batches days or even months ahead as needed.
sentences.zh: 例如，我们为一家能源密集型工业化学品公司实施了一项灵活的合同，其中包括现金支付安排，使他们能够在成本较低时锁定价格，并根据需要提前几天甚至几个月分批购买电力。

blocks.html_en: There are various kinds of flexible contract options based on the degree to which companies can forecast their energy consumption. For example, ‘tolerance banding’ which guarantees a set price within a certain range or ‘band’ of electricity consumption, can provide certainty amidst unpredictable, fluctuating costs.

sentences.zh: 根据公司对能源消耗的预测程度，有多种灵活的合同选项。

sentences.en: For example, ‘tolerance banding’ which guarantees a set price within a certain range or ‘band’ of electricity consumption, can provide certainty amidst unpredictable, fluctuating costs.
sentences.zh: 例如，“容差范围”保证了一定范围内的固定价格或用电量的“范围”，可以在不可预测、波动的成本中提供确定性。

blocks.html_en: As data center operators become more confident in forecasting energy consumption, this can create even cheaper options such as contracts that enable them to buy every kilowatt-hour above or below expected demand.

sentences.en: As data center operators become more confident in forecasting energy consumption, this can create even cheaper options such as contracts that enable them to buy every kilowatt-hour above or below expected demand.

sentences.en: As large consumers with a relatively stable long-term demand, data centers are perfectly placed to tap into this market.
sentences.zh: 作为长期需求相对稳定的大消费者，数据中心完全有能力开拓这个市场。

sentences.en: Amidst growing energy price volatility and security risks, fixed-price CPPAs offer the chance to lock in power prices and supplies, providing certainty and security.

sentences.zh: 替代合同的障碍

sentences.zh: 这些合同可能非常复杂，包含严格的信用要求，并且通常需要 10 至 15 年的协议。

sentences.en: The Contracts for Difference (CfD) scheme, which gives renewable developers a government-backed route to market through fixed long-term price support, can also act as a competing route to market for generators.
sentences.zh: 差价合约（CfD）计划通过固定的长期价格支持为可再生能源开发商提供了政府支持的市场途径，也可以作为发电机进入市场的竞争途径。

blocks.html_en: In some cases, this can make long-term corporate offtake agreements less attractive, particularly where developers can secure greater certainty through the CfD mechanism. For smaller customers seeking greener electricity, however, this route can offer an alternative when a direct CPPA may be harder to access.

sentences.en: In some cases, this can make long-term corporate offtake agreements less attractive, particularly where developers can secure greater certainty through the CfD mechanism.
sentences.zh: 在某些情况下，这可能会降低长期企业承购协议的吸引力，特别是在开发商可以通过差价合约机制获得更大确定性的情况下。

sentences.en: For smaller customers seeking greener electricity, however, this route can offer an alternative when a direct CPPA may be harder to access.
sentences.zh: 然而，对于寻求绿色电力的小客户来说，当直接 CPPA 可能难以获得时，这条路线可以提供替代方案。

blocks.html_en: Many data center projects are run by startups or smaller entities that lack the resources for such long-term commitments or credit requirements. Data centers also present a slightly higher credit risk than other facilities because their revenue streams are not based on a few large, long-term customers but split among many customers across the digital economy.

sentences.en: Many data center projects are run by startups or smaller entities that lack the resources for such long-term commitments or credit requirements.
sentences.zh: 许多数据中心项目是由初创公司或小型实体运营的，这些实体缺乏此类长期承诺或信贷要求的资源。

sentences.en: Data centers also present a slightly higher credit risk than other facilities because their revenue streams are not based on a few large, long-term customers but split among many customers across the digital economy.
sentences.zh: 数据中心的信用风险也比其他设施略高，因为它们的收入来源不是基于少数大型长期客户，而是分散在数字经济中的许多客户中。

blocks.html_en: Opening the market to data centers

sentences.en: Opening the market to data centers
sentences.zh: 向数据中心开放市场

blocks.html_en: There is an urgent need for creative solutions to lower barriers to entry to the flexible contract market for smaller entities such as data centers. Security deposits or bank guarantees can help some firms meet the stringent credit requirements.

sentences.en: There is an urgent need for creative solutions to lower barriers to entry to the flexible contract market for smaller entities such as data centers.
sentences.zh: 迫切需要创造性的解决方案来降低数据中心等小型实体进入灵活合同市场的壁垒。

sentences.en: Security deposits or bank guarantees can help some firms meet the stringent credit requirements.
sentences.zh: 保证金或银行担保可以帮助一些公司满足严格的信贷要求。

blocks.html_en: Other potential solutions include parent-company guarantees where a parent company makes a commitment to cover the cost in the event of a default or intercompany guarantees where large data center customers such as Amazon offer guarantees.

sentences.en: Other potential solutions include parent-company guarantees where a parent company makes a commitment to cover the cost in the event of a default or intercompany guarantees where large data center customers such as Amazon offer guarantees.
sentences.zh: 其他潜在的解决方案包括母公司担保（母公司承诺在发生违约时承担费用）或公司间担保（亚马逊等大型数据中心客户提供担保）。

blocks.html_en: There are also solutions to simplify adoption and help CPPAs slot into existing energy use. For example, PPA import sleeving contracts, where energy is pre-purchased from a generator or utility and supplied directly to a facility through the grid, can help alleviate energy costs and security risks.

sentences.en: There are also solutions to simplify adoption and help CPPAs slot into existing energy use.
sentences.zh: 还有一些解决方案可以简化采用并帮助 CPPA 融入现有的能源使用。

sentences.en: For example, PPA import sleeving contracts, where energy is pre-purchased from a generator or utility and supplied directly to a facility through the grid, can help alleviate energy costs and security risks.
sentences.zh: 例如，购电协议进口套管合同（即从发电机或公用事业公司预先购买能源并通过电网直接供应给设施）有助于降低能源成本和安全风险。

blocks.html_en: Tailoring contracts to specific energy needs

sentences.en: Tailoring contracts to specific energy needs
sentences.zh: 根据特定能源需求定制合同

blocks.html_en: Independent partners can also help data centers optimize contracts for their specific energy needs and financial situation. Independent brokers can consolidate the process of negotiating with generators, suppliers and investors, speeding up and de-risking adoption.

sentences.en: Independent partners can also help data centers optimize contracts for their specific energy needs and financial situation.
sentences.zh: 独立合作伙伴还可以帮助数据中心根据其特定的能源需求和财务状况优化合同。

sentences.en: Independent brokers can consolidate the process of negotiating with generators, suppliers and investors, speeding up and de-risking adoption.
sentences.zh: 独立经纪人可以整合与发电商、供应商和投资者的谈判过程，加快采用速度并降低风险。

blocks.html_en: Brokers can also help negotiate and monitor contracts suited to the precise profile of each data center. For example, cloud computing data centers with relatively steady, predictable demand may prefer fixed contracts whereas AI data centers processing huge amounts of data for many clients have a more variable, ‘peaky’ pattern of consumption and require flexible contracts.

sentences.en: Brokers can also help negotiate and monitor contracts suited to the precise profile of each data center.
sentences.zh: 经纪人还可以帮助协商和监控适合每个数据中心精确情况的合同。

sentences.en: For example, cloud computing data centers with relatively steady, predictable demand may prefer fixed contracts whereas AI data centers processing huge amounts of data for many clients have a more variable, ‘peaky’ pattern of consumption and require flexible contracts.
sentences.zh: 例如，需求相对稳定、可预测的云计算数据中心可能更喜欢固定合同，而为许多客户处理大量数据的人工智能数据中心则具有更加可变的“峰值”消费模式，需要灵活的合同。

blocks.html_en: Data centers can also work with partners to get live market intelligence on changing regulations or market movements. For example, we offered one client an early warning service so that they were able to minimize costs during the winter triads, half-hour periods of peak demand during the winter season.

sentences.en: Data centers can also work with partners to get live market intelligence on changing regulations or market movements.
sentences.zh: 数据中心还可以与合作伙伴合作，获取有关不断变化的法规或市场动向的实时市场情报。

sentences.en: For example, we offered one client an early warning service so that they were able to minimize costs during the winter triads, half-hour periods of peak demand during the winter season.
sentences.zh: 例如，我们为一位客户提供了预警服务，以便他们能够在冬季三合会（冬季需求高峰期的半小时）期间最大限度地降低成本。

blocks.html_en: Towards a new model of data center energy

sentences.en: Towards a new model of data center energy
sentences.zh: 迈向数据中心能源新模式

blocks.html_en: Britain’s ability to compete in the accelerating AI race increasingly hinges on the ability to mitigate the risk of rising energy costs.

sentences.en: Britain’s ability to compete in the accelerating AI race increasingly hinges on the ability to mitigate the risk of rising energy costs.
sentences.zh: 英国在日益加速的人工智能竞赛中的竞争能力越来越取决于降低能源成本上涨风险的能力。

blocks.html_en: Energy security will also be critical to building truly sovereign AI capabilities for the UK. Lowering the barriers to the flexible contract market could create new opportunities at both ends, providing secure, sustainable and affordable power to unlock data center growth while unlocking vital investment in new renewable energy capacity.

sentences.en: Energy security will also be critical to building truly sovereign AI capabilities for the UK.
sentences.zh: 能源安全对于英国建立真正主权的人工智能能力也至关重要。

sentences.en: Lowering the barriers to the flexible contract market could create new opportunities at both ends, providing secure, sustainable and affordable power to unlock data center growth while unlocking vital investment in new renewable energy capacity.
sentences.zh: 降低灵活合同市场的壁垒可以在两端创造新的机会，提供安全、可持续和负担得起的电力来释放数据中心的增长，同时释放对新的可再生能源容量的重要投资。

blocks.html_en: Yet this will require tailored, adaptable contractual models from bespoke flexible contracts to PPAs and new ways of lowering barriers to entry including reducing complexity and stringent credit requirements. This could help provide secure, sustainable and affordable long-term power to fuel our digital economy.

sentences.en: Yet this will require tailored, adaptable contractual models from bespoke flexible contracts to PPAs and new ways of lowering barriers to entry including reducing complexity and stringent credit requirements.
sentences.zh: 然而，这将需要定制的、适应性强的合同模式，从定制的灵活合同到购电协议，以及降低进入壁垒的新方法，包括降低复杂性和严格的信贷要求。

sentences.en: This could help provide secure, sustainable and affordable long-term power to fuel our digital economy.
sentences.zh: 这有助于提供安全、可持续且负担得起的长期电力来推动我们的数字经济。

blocks.html_en: We list the best web hosting services: 60 sites tested to find the 10 fastest, most reliable platforms.

sentences.en: We list the best web hosting services: 60 sites tested to find the 10 fastest, most reliable platforms.
sentences.zh: 我们列出了最好的网络托管服务：测试了 60 个站点，以找到 10 个最快、最可靠的平台。

sentences.en: This article was produced as part of TechRadar Pro Perspectives, our channel to feature the best and brightest minds in the technology industry today.
sentences.zh: 本文是 TechRadar Pro Perspectives 的一部分，这是我们的频道，旨在介绍当今技术行业中最优秀、最聪明的人才。

sentences.zh: 本文表达的观点仅代表作者的观点，并不一定代表 TechRadarPro 或 Future plc 的观点。

sentences.zh: 如果您有兴趣贡献，请在此处了解更多信息：https://www.techradar.com/pro/perspectives-how-to-submit

content_html_zh:
最近的证据表明，能源成本压力和电网容量紧缩有可能成为英国数字经济增长的瓶颈。

牛津经济研究院为核工业协会撰写的一份报告警告称，日益严重的电网容量限制和缺乏竞争力的工业电价可能会促使数据中心开发商转向海外。

到 2035 年，数据中心的能源需求可能会增加五倍，而数据中心运营商面临着越来越大的压力，要求提供符合气候目标的可持续能源。

SEFE Energy UK 的英国企业客户管理主管。

这促使许多数据中心开发商探索替代供应来源，例如灵活的合同和企业购电协议（CPPA），以提供负担得起的、安全的长期电力。

然而，许多数据中心开发商缺乏资源来处理这些复杂的合同或满足高信用要求。

迫切需要创造性的新解决方案，为我们的数字经济提供负担得起的可持续电力。

人工智能经济的能源瓶颈

政府的目标是使英国成为七国集团中采用人工智能最快的国家，这将需要加速数据中心的扩张，并优先考虑人工智能数据中心与电网的新需求连接。 虽然这值得欢迎，但需求方的连接并不能缓解供应方面临的挑战，如电力成本上升和网络容量限制等。

订阅 TechRadar Pro 时事通讯，获取您的企业成功所需的所有热门新闻、观点、功能和指导！

数据中心的建设时间为 12 至 14 个月，但大型新可再生能源项目可能需要等待 12 至 14 年才能上线，而此时英国面临非商品电力成本不断上涨，例如传输网络使用系统 (TNUoS) 和核监管资产基础 (RAB) 费用，以资助新的电网和核能基础设施。

Ofgem警告说，数据中心的电力消耗可能会大大超过英国目前的电力消耗峰值，从而带来能源成本上升的风险。

转向灵活合同

灵活的电力合同允许公司批量购买能源，这为数据中心根据其消耗量调整能源成本提供了一个潜在的解决方案。

至关重要的是，可以调整灵活的合同，以对冲人工智能数据中心等能源使用模式更加多样化的设施的能源消耗波动。 这也将有助于避免因增加能源使用量来吸引主要新客户而受到处罚。

虽然固定价格合同可以锁定长期能源成本以提供确定性，但灵活的合同使数据中心能够利用价格波动来获得更便宜的电力。

例如，我们有一个专门的定价团队全天候监控市场走势。 这可以帮助设施部分对冲价格上涨的风险，为部分消费设定价格，同时在需要时购买其余部分，以利用现货市场上的优惠价格。

例如，我们为一家能源密集型工业化学品公司实施了一项灵活的合同，其中包括现金支付安排，使他们能够在成本较低时锁定价格，并根据需要提前几天甚至几个月分批购买电力。

根据公司对能源消耗的预测程度，有多种灵活的合同选项。 例如，“容差范围”保证了一定范围内的固定价格或用电量的“范围”，可以在不可预测、波动的成本中提供确定性。

随着数据中心运营商对预测能源消耗变得更加有信心，这可以创造更便宜的选择，例如使他们能够购买高于或低于预期需求的每千瓦时的合同。

CPPA模型

CPPA 等其他合同可以直接从供应商以稳定的成本为数据中心提供安全、可持续、负担得起的电力供应。 涉及现场发电的专线购电协议可以使数据中心将剩余电力出售回电网，将能源从成本转变为收入流。 现场发电还可以帮助避免电网电力的非商品成本，例如 TNUoS 费用，该费用占电费的 60%，并且预计会增加，以资助新的基础设施。

CPPA 的目标是将建筑物的运营排放量减少 76%，还通过提供可追踪的绿色电力来帮助设施实现气候目标。 作为长期需求相对稳定的大消费者，数据中心完全有能力开拓这个市场。 在能源价格波动和安全风险日益加剧的情况下，固定价格 CPPA 提供了锁定电价和供应的机会，从而提供确定性和安全性。

替代合同的障碍

然而，数据中心等小型实体的灵活合同和 CPPA 市场存在许多障碍。 这些合同可能非常复杂，包含严格的信用要求，并且通常需要 10 至 15 年的协议。 差价合约（CfD）计划通过固定的长期价格支持为可再生能源开发商提供了政府支持的市场途径，也可以作为发电机进入市场的竞争途径。

在某些情况下，这可能会降低长期企业承购协议的吸引力，特别是在开发商可以通过差价合约机制获得更大确定性的情况下。 然而，对于寻求绿色电力的小客户来说，当直接 CPPA 可能难以获得时，这条路线可以提供替代方案。

许多数据中心项目是由初创公司或小型实体运营的，这些实体缺乏此类长期承诺或信贷要求的资源。 数据中心的信用风险也比其他设施略高，因为它们的收入来源不是基于少数大型长期客户，而是分散在数字经济中的许多客户中。

向数据中心开放市场

迫切需要创造性的解决方案来降低数据中心等小型实体进入灵活合同市场的壁垒。 保证金或银行担保可以帮助一些公司满足严格的信贷要求。

其他潜在的解决方案包括母公司担保（母公司承诺在发生违约时承担费用）或公司间担保（亚马逊等大型数据中心客户提供担保）。

还有一些解决方案可以简化采用并帮助 CPPA 融入现有的能源使用。 例如，购电协议进口套管合同（即从发电机或公用事业公司预先购买能源并通过电网直接供应给设施）有助于降低能源成本和安全风险。

根据特定能源需求定制合同

独立合作伙伴还可以帮助数据中心根据其特定的能源需求和财务状况优化合同。 独立经纪人可以整合与发电商、供应商和投资者的谈判过程，加快采用速度并降低风险。

经纪人还可以帮助协商和监控适合每个数据中心精确情况的合同。 例如，需求相对稳定、可预测的云计算数据中心可能更喜欢固定合同，而为许多客户处理大量数据的人工智能数据中心则具有更加可变的“峰值”消费模式，需要灵活的合同。

数据中心还可以与合作伙伴合作，获取有关不断变化的法规或市场动向的实时市场情报。 例如，我们为一位客户提供了预警服务，以便他们能够在冬季三合会（冬季需求高峰期的半小时）期间最大限度地降低成本。

迈向数据中心能源新模式

英国在日益加速的人工智能竞赛中的竞争能力越来越取决于降低能源成本上涨风险的能力。

能源安全对于英国建立真正主权的人工智能能力也至关重要。 降低灵活合同市场的壁垒可以在两端创造新的机会，提供安全、可持续和负担得起的电力来释放数据中心的增长，同时释放对新的可再生能源容量的重要投资。

然而，这将需要定制的、适应性强的合同模式，从定制的灵活合同到购电协议，以及降低进入壁垒的新方法，包括降低复杂性和严格的信贷要求。 这有助于提供安全、可持续且负担得起的长期电力来推动我们的数字经济。

我们列出了最好的网络托管服务：测试了 60 个站点，以找到 10 个最快、最可靠的平台。

本文是 TechRadar Pro Perspectives 的一部分，这是我们的频道，旨在介绍当今技术行业中最优秀、最聪明的人才。
